lead_profile_text: Property developer exploring STL as alternative to social/council leasing. Typically acquires sites, converts to flats, leases whole buildings to housing associations or councils. In Bridlington, no existing HA/council connections. Actual project: burned-down hotel in Bridlington area, potential for ~20 apartment conversion. Acquisition in negotiation — at least 1 year from completion (planning + construction). Address above is analyser reference unit (2-bed in YO15). Ben has 3-4 other active UK projects. Call quality very poor (Ben on train) — switched to phone.
emotional_profile: Commercial developer mindset — purely ROI-focused. Patient and long-term. Initially wanted master lease (not possible); accepted management fee model quickly. Open to Stayful for future projects nationwide. Poor call quality may have reduced engagement.
delay_trigger_phrases: I will keep you posted on how the deal goes
delay_timeline: At least 1 year (acquisition negotiations, planning, construction required before any STL launch)

top_objections.text: Is someone able to rent the whole building at a fixed monthly rate?
top_objections.zac_response: Not Stayful's model. Confirmed alternative: Stayful manages on Ben's behalf and takes a management fee from revenue. Variable income model accepted.

top_objections.text: Will putting 20 units in the STL market flood local demand?
top_objections.zac_response: Phased approach: top floor on STL first, remaining on LTL, expand floor by floor if demand holds. Stop at optimal STL/LTL mix.

top_objections.text: How accurate is the Stayful analyser?
top_objections.zac_response: Confirmed accuracy; highlighted low competition in the Bridlington area as a positive signal for returns.

top_questions.text: Can Stayful lease the whole building at a fixed monthly rate?
top_questions.answer: No — Stayful's model is management-based. They manage on the owner's behalf and take a management fee from revenue. Variable income, not fixed lease.

top_questions.text: Will putting 20 units into STL flood demand in this area?
top_questions.answer: Phased rollout recommended: top floor on STL first, remaining on LTL, expand floor by floor if demand holds. Stop at optimal STL/LTL mix.
Yes — relevant because Ben has 3-4 other active projects across the UK.

what_moved_them: Phased floor-by-floor rollout strategy — 'I understand what you can offer now'
Low competition signal in Bridlington area — viewed as a positive
Stayful nationwide coverage — directly relevant given his other active UK projects
what_fell_flat: Master lease not available — main initial request denied (though accepted quickly by Ben)
Poor call quality on train — likely reduced engagement and depth of conversation

conversion_gaps.gap: Project at least 1 year from launch — no near-term conversion opportunity on this site
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Check in every 2 months on acquisition progress. Offer to run Stayful analyser on other UK projects by postcode to build relationship.

conversion_gaps.gap: Acquisition still in negotiation — no certainty of site proceeding
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Position Stayful as preferred operator for when acquisition completes. Keep relationship warm with periodic analyser runs.

conversion_gaps.gap: 3-4 other active UK projects may be nearer-term opportunities
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Ask about other projects in follow-up — offer to run Stayful analyser on each postcode to test STL viability vs HA/council leasing

offer_framing: No offer made. Long-term relationship — focus on nationwide capability and phased rollout differentiation. Revisit with offer when project nears completion.
zac_close_phrase: Touch base in a couple of months
zac_income_intro: Per 2-bed reference unit in YO15 (Bridlington): Annual STL gross £32,436 / net £16,865 (£1,405/month average). Range £771 (Nov) to £2,703 (Aug). Potential after 1yr on platform: ~£1,900/month net. LTL monthly gross £1,485 / net after agent ~£990. STL uplift vs LTL net: +42%.
zac_reassurance_phrases: Phased approach — top floor on STL first, remaining on LTL, expand floor by floor if demand holds
Low competition in the area confirmed by the analyser — positive signal for returns
Stayful operates nationwide — relevant for Ben's other active projects
zac_analogies: Floor-by-floor phased rollout — test demand before committing to full STL across all 20 units
